page: Amazon Web Services c9g.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 64 dedicated physical cores with a clock speed of 2.8 GHz and a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, meaning there is no simultaneous multithreading. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which offloads virtualization functions to dedicated hardware. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 34 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance type.

The arm64 architecture offers qualitative cost efficiency and power savings compared to traditional x86 hardware, though it requires software compiled specifically for ARM. The low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, positioning this instance primarily for compute-bound workloads. Appropriate use cases include batch processing, distributed analytics, web serving, and video encoding.

tagline: A dedicated 64-core arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring a 1:2 core-to-memory ratio and 34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
